title: Fancy Hotbar
description: Adds a Lilypad-like hotbar which looks fancy
body:
This texture pack is a small visual change made purely for style. It replaces the default Minecraft hotbar with a custom lilypad-themed design that looks cleaner, softer, and a bit more fancy than vanilla. If you like subtle UI tweaks that don’t mess with gameplay, this pack is made exactly for that.

The idea behind the lilypad hotbar is simple: keep Minecraft’s original feel, but add a calm and aesthetic touch to the screen. The hotbar slots are designed to resemble lilypads floating on water, using smooth shapes, natural green tones, and gentle shading. It doesn’t try to be flashy or distracting — it just makes the UI feel nicer to look at during normal gameplay.

This pack only changes the hotbar, nothing else. No blocks, no items, no sounds. Because of that, it’s very lightweight and performance-friendly. You can use it on low-end PCs, with shaders, or alongside other texture packs without worrying about lag or compatibility issues. It also works well when stacked with other resource packs, since it focuses on just one small part of the UI.

The design keeps visibility and readability in mind. Item slots are still clear, and you won’t struggle to see what you’re holding while fighting, mining, or building. It’s meant to feel natural — like something that could exist in the game, just with a bit more personality.

This texture pack is great for casual players who want a nicer interface, as well as content creators who want their gameplay to look a bit more unique in screenshots or videos. It’s a tiny change, but one you’ll notice every time you play.

If you’re someone who enjoys aesthetic details and clean UI design, this lilypad hotbar is a simple way to make Minecraft feel more personal without changing how the game works at all.
